gas.tip: 💡 Verified via GasBuddy. Costco requires membership — split with 3 friends and it pays for itself in 2 fill-ups.

food.tip: 💡 Top Dog cash only — FAWN’s $0.01 fee beats the ATM every time.

coffee.tip: 💡 Brewed Awakening is open latest and rarely full during finals.

housing.tip: 💡 Albany & El Cerrito are 15 min by BART — cheapest options near campus.

bars.tip: 💡 Kip’s runs $3 drafts on weeknights until 9 PM.

bulk.tip: 💡 BevMo’s 5¢ sale stacks on wine — best deal in the East Bay.

items: Cal Student Store — 10% off w/ Cal 1 Card
coupons.tip: 💡 Stack with UNiDAYS/Student Beans — see Student Perks tab for verified online codes.

items: Diamond Gas & Mart, E Evelyn Ave (Mtn View)
Rotten Robbie, N Whisman Rd (Mtn View)
gas.tip: 💡 Verified via GasBuddy. Diamond Gas is the cheapest within 5 miles of campus.

food.tip: 💡 Tacos El Grullense in Redwood City = best $10 meal near campus.

coffee.tip: 💡 Red Rock Coffee has the most open seats for studying off-campus.

housing.tip: 💡 East Palo Alto is 10 min by bike — biggest savings near Stanford.

bars.tip: 💡 Antonio’s is the cheapest bar in Palo Alto — cash preferred.

bulk.tip: 💡 Total Wine Sunnyvale has the best selection and price on the Peninsula.

items: Caltrain Go Pass discount for students
coupons.tip: 💡 Caltrain student discount alone can save $40+/month if you commute.

items: Excel Gas & Mart, W Evelyn Ave (Sunnyvale)
gas.tip: 💡 Verified via GasBuddy. The San Jose Costco is about 10 minutes away and worth the membership.

food.tip: 💡 In-N-Out is a 5 min drive — best calorie-per-dollar on this list.

coffee.tip: 💡 Caffino has the most reliable wifi and outlets near campus.

housing.tip: 💡 San Jose near the light rail is the cheapest option — 20 min to campus.

bars.tip: 💡 Mission City Bar is the go-to for cheap drinks near SCU.

bulk.tip: 💡 Costco Kirkland vodka is legitimately good — same distillery as Grey Goose.

items: SCU Bookstore — 10% off w/ Bronco Card
coupons.tip: 💡 The VTA Eco Pass through SCU is often near-free — check with student services.

gas.tip: 💡 Verified via GasBuddy. Poppy on Keyes St is the closest cheap option to downtown SJSU.

food.tip: 💡 La Victoria’s burrito is genuinely a full meal for under $7.

coffee.tip: 💡 Roy’s Station is a 10-min walk but has the cheapest pour-over downtown.

housing.tip: 💡 Light rail access cuts your commute cost and opens up cheaper rooms further out.

bars.tip: 💡 Singlebarrel’s happy hour runs until 7 PM most weekdays.

bulk.tip: 💡 Almaden Total Wine restocks the 30-pack deal weekly — check Thursdays.

items: SJSU Spartan Bookstore — 15% off supplies w/ ID
coupons.tip: 💡 Show your Tower Card at the Spartan Bookstore for the supply discount — often unadvertised.

gas.tip: 💡 Verified via GasBuddy. SF itself rarely has stations under $5.50 — the South SF Costco is the closest real discount, worth the drive if you have a membership.

food.tip: 💡 Anda Piroshki on Fulton is the best $5 meal within walking distance.

coffee.tip: 💡 Simple Pleasures has live jazz and the cheapest cup in the Richmond.

housing.tip: 💡 Outer Sunset is a longer Muni ride but consistently the cheapest SF option near USF.

items: Beach Chalet — pints w/ ocean view
bars.tip: 💡 Mauna Loa’s happy hour is the best value-to-vibe ratio near campus.

bulk.tip: 💡 Nearest Total Wine is in Daly City — worth the BART ride for a group buy.

items: USF Bookstore — 10% off w/ USF ID
Clipper Card student discount on Muni
coupons.tip: 💡 The Muni student discount through Clipper adds up fast if you commute daily.

items: Costco, El Camino Real (South SF, closest Costco)
gas.tip: 💡 Verified via GasBuddy. South SF Costco is a quick drive and worth it if you have a membership — SF gas itself runs well above $5.50.

food.tip: 💡 Imperial Garden’s lunch special is the best value sit-down meal near campus.

items: Devil's Teeth Baking Co — coffee + pastry
coffee.tip: 💡 Devil's Teeth gets crowded weekends — go on a weekday morning.

housing.tip: 💡 Parkside and Ingleside are both walkable to campus and cheaper than the Sunset.

bars.tip: 💡 Riptide is the classic SF State after-class spot — cheap and close.

bulk.tip: 💡 Colma’s Total Wine is the best bulk run for a group house near SF State.

items: SF State Bookstore — 10% off w/ Gator ID
coupons.tip: 💡 Stack the Clipper student discount with a monthly pass for the biggest savings.

items: Speedway Express, Ygnacio Valley Rd (Walnut Creek)
gas.tip: 💡 Verified via GasBuddy. Walnut Creek is the closest cheap gas, about 15 minutes from Moraga.

food.tip: 💡 La Finestra runs a weekday lunch special that beats most fast food nearby.

coffee.tip: 💡 Diablo Coffee in Lafayette is a short drive and the best study spot in the area.

housing.tip: 💡 Walnut Creek has the most affordable rooms with easy BART access.

items: Walnut Creek bars — happy hour pints
bars.tip: 💡 Lafayette and Walnut Creek have far more nightlife options than Moraga itself.

bulk.tip: 💡 Walnut Creek has the nearest Total Wine and BevMo — worth a group trip.

items: SMC Bookstore — 10% off w/ Gael Card
Walnut Creek movie theater student rate
coupons.tip: 💡 The County Connection student pass is the cheapest way to reach Walnut Creek and BART.

gas.tip: 💡 Verified via GasBuddy. Eugene gas still runs noticeably cheaper than the Bay Area — enjoy it.

food.tip: 💡 Track Town Pizza is a UO institution — $5.50 is unbeatable.

coffee.tip: 💡 Wandering Goat roasts in-house and is the best study spot near 5th Street Market.

housing.tip: 💡 Eugene is one of the most affordable college towns in the country. Friendly St is the move.

bars.tip: 💡 $2 drafts at Max’s on Monday nights — cheapest bar night in Eugene.

bulk.tip: 💡 BiMart is Oregon’s secret weapon — $14.99 handles that you won’t find anywhere else.

items: Duck Store — student discount w/ ID
coupons.tip: 💡 The LTD bus pass is already included in most UO tuition — check before paying for parking.

gas.tip: 💡 Verified via GasBuddy. Maverik on Sunset Blvd is the cheapest in Rocklin, a short drive from campus.

food.tip: 💡 In-N-Out Rocklin is a 5-minute drive from Sierra College and the best value combo around.

items: Local roaster near Old Town Roseville
coffee.tip: 💡 Dudley's has the most reliable wifi and outlets for studying off-campus.

housing.tip: 💡 Roseville has more rental inventory and slightly lower prices than Rocklin proper.

bars.tip: 💡 This is a commuter-college area — nightlife is light, but the local breweries have solid happy hours.

bulk.tip: 💡 Costco Rocklin is a straight shot down Sierra College Blvd from campus.

items: Sierra College Bookstore — discount w/ ID
coupons.tip: 💡 Sierra College students get a reduced Placer County Transit fare — ask at student services.

gas.tip: 💡 Verified via GasBuddy. The Power Inn Rd Sam’s Club is the closest warehouse option to campus.

food.tip: 💡 Tower Cafe near the Tower Theatre is a Sac State institution for a sit-down lunch.

coffee.tip: 💡 Naked Lounge has the latest hours and the most study-friendly seating near campus.

housing.tip: 💡 College Greens is the classic Sac State student neighborhood — walkable and the cheapest nearby option.

bars.tip: 💡 Old Ironsides runs one of the most reliable happy hours in midtown Sacramento.

bulk.tip: 💡 Total Wine on Howe Ave is the easiest bulk run from campus.

items: Hornet Bookstore — discount w/ Sac State ID
coupons.tip: 💡 The SacRT student pass makes commuting from cheaper neighborhoods actually worth it.

items: Quick N Save, N Beale Rd (Linda)
Sam's Club, N Walton Ave (Yuba City)
gas.tip: 💡 Verified via GasBuddy. Quick N Save is consistently the cheapest gas for 50+ miles around.

food.tip: 💡 The taco trucks along Colusa Ave are the best value lunch in town.

coffee.tip: 💡 Dutch Bros is the fastest option if you’re commuting in before class.

housing.tip: 💡 This is one of the most affordable college towns on this whole list — rents run far below the state average.

items: Yuba City sports bars — happy hour
bars.tip: 💡 Nightlife is limited here — most options are downtown Marysville, a short drive from campus.

bulk.tip: 💡 WinCo consistently beats the chain grocery stores on bulk beer prices here.

items: Yuba College Bookstore — discount w/ ID
coupons.tip: 💡 Yuba-Sutter Transit offers a reduced student fare — ask at the registrar.

items: Conserv Fuel, Broad St (San Luis Obispo)
San Paso Truck Stop (Paso Robles, ~30 min)
gas.tip: 💡 Verified via GasBuddy. Conserv Fuel on Broad St is the closest cheap option to campus.

food.tip: 💡 Firestone Grill is THE Cal Poly institution — the tri-tip sandwich is worth the line.

coffee.tip: 💡 Linnaea's has a backyard patio that's the best hidden study spot downtown.

housing.tip: 💡 SLO is a notoriously expensive college town — locking in roommates early matters more here than almost anywhere on this list.

bars.tip: 💡 Mother's Tavern happy hour is the best value night out near campus.

bulk.tip: 💡 The Paso Robles Costco run is worth it once a month if you’re splitting with roommates.

items: El Corral Bookstore — discount w/ PolyCard
coupons.tip: 💡 The SLO Transit student pass is worth it if you live off the Cal Poly bus loop.

gas.tip: 💡 Verified via GasBuddy. The Russell Blvd ARCO is the closest cheap gas to campus.

food.tip: 💡 The Davis Co-op deli is consistently the best value lunch a few blocks from campus.

coffee.tip: 💡 Mishka's has the most study seating and stays open latest near downtown Davis.

housing.tip: 💡 Davis is famously bike-friendly — living further from campus costs less and barely adds commute time.

bars.tip: 💡 G Street WunderBar’s happy hour is the standard Davis student move.

bulk.tip: 💡 Nearest Costco/Total Wine bulk runs are a quick drive into Sacramento or Woodland.

items: UC Davis Bookstore — discount w/ student ID
coupons.tip: 💡 Unitrans is usually already covered by your student fees — check before buying a separate pass.

gas.tip: 💡 Verified via GasBuddy. Alliance on N Fremont St is the cheapest verified option near campus.

food.tip: 💡 Turtle Bay Taqueria is the best value sit-down meal near the Seaside/CSUMB campus.

coffee.tip: 💡 Skip the Cannery Row tourist-priced cafes when possible — Seaside options run noticeably cheaper.

housing.tip: 💡 The Monterey Peninsula is one of the most expensive housing markets on this list — Marina tends to run cheaper than Seaside or Monterey itself.

bars.tip: 💡 Alvarado Street Brewery has the best happy hour value in downtown Monterey.

bulk.tip: 💡 Costco Seaside is the most direct bulk run from CSUMB.

items: Otter Bookstore — discount w/ CSUMB ID
coupons.tip: 💡 The MST student pass is worth it if you commute in from Salinas or Marina.

items: Conserv Fuel, La Cumbre Rd (Santa Barbara)
gas.tip: 💡 Verified via GasBuddy. Summit Gasoline is the cheapest verified option, with a cash discount on top.

food.tip: 💡 Freebirds is the defining Isla Vista meal — a burrito here is a full day of food for under $10.

coffee.tip: 💡 French Press is the classic IV study spot, a block from campus.

housing.tip: 💡 IV is notoriously expensive and overcrowded — Goleta a few miles out runs meaningfully cheaper.

items: The Study at IV (non-alc grind spot)
bars.tip: 💡 Most IV social life is house parties rather than bars — downtown Santa Barbara is the move for an actual bar night.

bulk.tip: 💡 Costco Goleta is the closest warehouse bulk run from IV/campus.

items: UCSB Bookstore — discount w/ Access Card
coupons.tip: 💡 The MTD bus pass is usually already covered by UCSB student fees — check before paying twice.

gas.tip: 💡 Verified via GasBuddy. Towne Pump is the cheapest verified option, a short drive from campus.

food.tip: 💡 The downtown Corvallis food carts are the best value lunch within walking distance of campus.

coffee.tip: 💡 New Morning Bakery pairs the best pastry with your coffee of anywhere downtown.

housing.tip: 💡 Corvallis is meaningfully cheaper than most California schools on this list — south Corvallis has the best value.

bars.tip: 💡 Block 15 is the defining Corvallis brewery — worth the regular price even off happy hour.

bulk.tip: 💡 WinCo consistently beats the grocery chains on bulk beer prices here, same as the rest of the Willamette Valley.

items: OSU Beaver Store — discount w/ student ID
CTS (Corvallis Transit System) student bus pass
coupons.tip: 💡 CTS bus service is already free for OSU students — don’t pay for parking if you don’t need to.
